title: Xtra Silky Peruvian Portobello Mushrooms Anticuchos
description: A sweet and silky Peruvian dish featuring shaoxing wine and lamination-puff, perfect for a monsoon evening, this ovo-vegetarian-friendly recipe is a twist on traditional anticuchos.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together shaoxing wine, soy sauce, brown sugar, garlic, and ginger.
Add the portobello mushrooms to the bowl and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Roll out the lamination puff pastry to a large sheet, about 1/4 inch thick.
Cut the pastry into 12 equal pieces, about 1 inch wide and 3 inches long.
Wrap each mushroom cap with a piece of pastry, brushing the edges with beaten egg to seal.
Place the anticuchos on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each.
Brush the tops of the pastry with vegetable oil and season with salt and black pepper.
Bake the anticuchos in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
Serve the anticuchos hot, garnished with chopped scallions and a side of your favorite dipping sauce.
